author: tylerhuntington222
text: In the example MaterialsBuilder script under 'Running a Builder' in the root directory's README I'm trying to understand why the `MongoStore` constructor receives the `collection_name="materials"` kwarg for instantiating the `tasks_store` object, and `collection_name="tasks"` for instantiating `materials_store`. Should these parameters be swapped? Or am I misunderstanding the conceptual/functional role of each Store instance?

author: mkhorton
text:
Yes, they should probably be swapped. The readme was written very early on before much of the builders themselves were written and could probably be updated. Certainly, some simpler examples could be added now (we try to use the MapBuilder base class as much as possible, but I don’t think that’s in the readme).

What’s your use case?

author: dwinston
text: [@mkhorton](https://github.com/mkhorton) Tyler's "use case" is helping us. He's one of the MP part-timers starting this month. :)

author: tylerhuntington222
text: [@mkhorton](https://github.com/mkhorton) thanks for the clarification! The script makes a lot more sense with those parameters switched. And, yes, right now just trying to orient myself to the MP codebase so I can help out over the next few months.
